design_philosophy.core_loop: Pitch/hit decision-making cycle with turn-based batting and pitching, managing team abilities across exhibition or season play
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Pitcher vs. batter dynamic with adjustable pre-game difficulty bias; risk/reward of swing timing and pitch selection
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Manage and compete with a real MLB team (with fictional rosters) across a full season, endorsed by celebrity athlete Tommy Lasorda
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Arcade-styled baseball prioritizing action and quick gameplay over full simulation; deliberately simplifies MLB rules while retaining core mechanics; offers pre-game difficulty tuning favoring pitcher, batter, or balanced play

difficulty.checkpointing: Password system enables season progression persistence and World Series tracking
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: pre-game difficulty setting biases gameplay toward pitcher or batter; players control fielding error frequency and environmental hazards (wind); AI opponents noted as difficult, potentially deterring novice players

progression.upgrade_system: Team and player ability management: batters rated by batting average, home runs, speed, fielding, throwing; pitchers rated by ERA, stamina, curve-throwing, speed, hit distance
progression.unlock_mechanism: 30-game season structure with password system for persistence; unlock progression through World Series completion
replayability_hooks: 26 teams with real city names enable multiple season runs
Season play with password saves for non-linear session breaks

world_building_techniques: Real MLB team city names establish authenticity (though rosters are fictional)
Tommy Lasorda celebrity endorsement and in-game presence as marquee figure
Arcade cabinet presence in Sega Mega-Tech emphasizes competitive arcade culture

faction_indicators: 26 distinct MLB-inspired teams with city identities
Pitcher vs. batter skill specialization encourages role-based play in multiplayer
danger_signals: Difficult AI noted by reviewers as potential frustration point for casual players
Luck-based gameplay criticized; randomization in pitch outcomes and fielding errors
environmental_storytelling: Realistic sprite graphics and animations communicate MLB authenticity
Tommy Lasorda's image and endorsement signal celebrity sports crossover appeal
Season structure with league tracking implies persistent competitive hierarchy

sources_used: Wikipedia: Tommy Lasorda Baseball (gameplay, release, reception, design philosophy)
